tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer sought advanced embedded signing features with OTP and email invite flexibility, faced product limitations especially with Greek character support in preset signatures, and received detailed but non-resolving responses from the AI Agent and teammate, leading to high customer effort and negative product feedback.
tickets.body: Hello signNow Support / API Team,           We are implementing embedded signing via API (embedded invites + embedded signing links).             Our requirements are:              Create embedded signing links for signers with OTP authentication (e.g., SMS).     At a later time, we want the ability to trigger signNow to send an email invitation to only selected signers, without changing the already issued embedded link(s) (i.e., keep the same signing URL that the signer has).    Ideally, this “send email later” action should be possible per signer (not to all recipients at once), and should not require re-creating the invite.             Question:    Is it technically possible to start with embedded signing (link delivery) and later send signNow email invitations for specific signers without regenerating / changing the signing link?          Thank you.            Kind regards,     Michos Ioannis
tickets.subject: Embedded signing links with OTP + later email sending for selected signers without changing links

 EMPLOYEE FORMS 
 1. Employee Application Form    • Personal Information    • Employment History    • Education    • Certifications    • References    • Driver's License Information    • Vehicle Information    • Availability    • Resume Upload    • Electronic Signature

You said: The Open-Vision of The Green Plant, Sprouting UP from Beneath The Soil. what do this means biblically?The Open-Vision of The Green Plant, Sprouting UP from Beneath The Soil. what do this means biblically? +17 An open vision of a green plant sprouting up from beneath the soil biblically signifies spiritual resurrection, the materialization of hidden prayers, and a sudden transition into a season of fruitful breakthrough.In scripture, agricultural life is a foundational metaphor used by God to communicate the hidden dynamics of the heart and the sudden manifestation of His promises.     1. Resurrection and Life Conquering DarknessA sprouting green plant is a core symbol of the Resurrection of Jesus Christ. Jesus compared Himself to a seed that must fall into the earth and die before it can rise to produce much fruit (John 12:24). Breaking through the soil symbolizes the power of life conquering the darkness of the grave.The Application: Something you thought was completely dead, buried, or forgotten is being brought back to life by the Holy Spirit.     2. The Manifestation of Buried WorkSoil represents the hidden incubation period where God works deep within a person's life, out of sight. The physical sprout represents the moment that which was hidden is finally revealed.The Application: Your "waiting season" is coming to an end. The prayers, tears, and faithfulness you invested in secret are bursting through the surface into a tangible, visible reality.     3. A Prophetic "Sprout of Righteousness"In the Old Testament, God frequently refers to His promises and the Messiah as a "Sprout" or a "Shoot" emerging from seemingly dead stumps or dry ground (Isaiah 11:1, Jeremiah 33:15). Isaiah 61:11 states, "For as the soil makes the sprout come up... so the Sovereign Lord will make righteousness and praise spring up before all nations.".The Application: This is a sign of divine favor, restoration, and rapid spiritual acceleration.     4. The Color Green: Vitality and HopeThe color green biblically represents hope, spiritual renewal, life, and the transformative power of the Holy Spirit. It signals that your life is entering a healthy state of thriving, leaving behind dry or barren conditions.To better understand this specific revelation, consider these reflection questions:Did the soil look dry and rocky, or rich and fertile?     What specific area of your life (finances, a relationship, a ministry) has felt "buried" or dead lately? tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer experienced production degradations impacting clients and sought root cause and monitoring options. The AI Agent provided monitoring info but could not address root cause, prompting human teammate involvement who delivered a detailed explanation and addressed follow-up concerns.
tickets.body:
Good morning SignNow Team,    I hope you are doing well.    We are reaching out to better understand the recent production degradations we have experienced with SignNow, which have impacted our clients' ability to sign documents successfully.    While we are actively enhancing our own systems to handle these scenarios more gracefully, we would appreciate any insight you can provide regarding the root causes of these incidents. Additionally, we would like to know whether SignNow offers any status notifications, monitoring feeds, webhooks, or alerting mechanisms that we could leverage within our own monitoring and incident response processes.    As we continue onboarding new clients, the reliability of the document-signing experience is critically important. Repeated service disruptions can affect user confidence and create challenges in assuring clients of a seamless signing process. Any guidance on how best to monitor SignNow service health or proactively respond to platform issues would be greatly appreciated.           
          Thank you for your time and assistance. We look forward to your response.             Taron R Kondakindi      Director, Software Development      He/Him      M:   617 852 2345       taron.kondakindi@extremereach.com        XR.GLOBAL
tickets.subject: Inquiry Regarding Recent Production Degradations and Monitoring Options

tickets.body:
Jay Active 
  Hi there! 👋 I'm Luna, your AI Agent. What can I help you with? Luna • AI Agent • 31m Very unhappy> need billing support live • 31m Feel free to ask your question below. Luna • AI Agent • 31m I understand your frustration, and I will connect you with someone from our team who can assist you with billing. Meanwhile, could you please share any additional details about your issue to help them assist you more efficiently? Luna • AI Agent • 31m Need to talk to a billing agent I just a paid you 200$ for my account. Need to talk to a person • 31m The team will get back to you on this. Our usual response time is approximately 2 hours. You'll get replies directly to the chatbot and to  rbjpholdings@gmail.com . Luna • AI Agent • 30m Profile image for Jay Jay joined the conversation Greetings from SignNow Support! My name is Jay. I'll be assisting you today. How can I help you? Jay • 29m Pass this along. I paid for the 15$ monthly trick for pdf filer before the signnow as you have set it up so underhandedly. Then I pay the monthly, you charge me for the year without my permission. AND the api link is still not active. Not happy. Before I contact my credit card company, cancel your service, leave my bad experience in reviews • 28m Can we straighten this out • 28m I see. Are you referring to the PDFFiller plan bundled with SignNow? Jay • 28m You conveniently make that look as your payment option. Then I paid for the monthly strictly made sure it was on the monthly and the billing system charged me for the yearly on the main sign now so now I'm not $200 and it's still not working • 27m out the 200 • 27m The reason why I am asking is because if you have already purchased a PDFFiller with a SignNow bundle, you do not need to buy a separate SignNow plan. And if you have indeed purchased a PDFFiller bundle plan, it’s possible that you may have accidentally purchased a separate SignNow plan for this account ( rbjpholdings@gmail.com ). Because I can see a scheduled plan set to activate on June 8, 2026. Jay • 25m No. You charged me that 15 then the 180 for your reg service • 20m I am now 200 in • 20m Okay just to make sure. So your PDFFiller plan is a totally separate purchase from your SignNow plan? Jay • 19m 
  Yea you get me for both • 18m But I choose the monthly you charged me for the year and its still not working • 17m The only thing I see here on our end is a scheduled purchase of SignNow Business plan Monthly. This starts on June 8, 2026. Jay • 16m That other payment you are showing is for PDFFiller, not SignNow. Jay • 16m What is the 200 in charges • 9m Do you want to see them? What is the plan i just pasted • 9m Are you still there • 4m HELLO ​

After evaluating our operational needs, I decided to move to a more complete platform that combines document storage, client portal, tax preparation workflow, and electronic signature features in a single system. tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer expressed strong negative sentiment about product limitations and experienced high effort due to unresolved reporting issues. The AI Agent provided low-quality responses, and the teammate did not resolve the issue.
tickets.body:
Good Morning,    If whoever is reading this email is unable to help with my request, please escalate to a manager or senior tech.    We have a financial manager that collaborates with another financial team at our organization. He doesn’t send docs for signature but needs to look at them but this is not possible without Admin rights which we don’t want to give out to someone who is not an admin.    I am an admin and can see the reports but SignNow has SO many limitations. Here are a few:      In document reports, it allows you to pick a data range for a year (for example), but it doesn’t display or export a years’ worth of data.  
 You can’t search for multiple criteria in any filter section except for the status. 
 I searched for document names beginning with FY250 but it showed no results. 
    So I connected with support, Lena told me to let her know what I needed and it sounded like she could run the report for me but what she sent wasn’t anything remotely close to what I had asked. I’m told these are the limitations of SignNow but I’m pretty sure there must be some way on your end to be able to query the actual database and provide us with this report so that we can do what we need to for our business without spending all day long running reports a month at a time. By the way it looks on my end, even if we did that, we would not be able find any documents that begin with FY250.    I can’t imagine a company like yours would just leave us hanging like this.     This is what we are looking for:     We want a report that starts on March 1, 2025 until today and filter for Document Names that start with FY250 & FY260 (the filter doesn’t let you use more than 1 criteria per filter section which is another big limitation).   We only need to see the statuses Pending and Complete.   We would like a report like this every 2 weeks after that for just these records since the last report but if this part is not possible at least one good report would help.    Please do the right thing, get me in tough with a real manager and let’s get this thing resolved.      Nelson Pidgeon       Project Coordinator, Web     Boston Public Health Commission     617-534-2854 (office)     617-275-1764 (cell)      npidgeon@bphc.org

I look forward to a written and final response.     Best regards,     Giada Gargano

tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer positively values the signNow platform but is frustrated by the account suspension under Section 5.2 of the Terms of Service. Despite providing detailed verification and explanations, the teammate upheld the suspension, leaving the issue unresolved.
tickets.body:
Dear signNow Support Team,  
  I am writing to appeal the suspension of my account associated with the email address   vvsolod@gmail.com              , which was flagged for a Section 5.2 Terms of Service violation. Additionally, I am currently unable to log into my other account,   slava@nansen.id              .       
 I recently switched to signNow from DocuSign and have been thoroughly enjoying your platform. However, I believe my account was mistakenly flagged by your automated anti-spam/anti-fraud system. 
 Here is the context of the activity that likely triggered the suspension: 
 
   I was sending a legitimate personal legal document titled "20260528 Acknowledgement of Outstanding Obligations"             to a group of my actual creditors.        
   To streamline the process, I sent the signing request to my own email address and placed the recipients in the CC field            .        
   The cover message contained external Google Drive links providing proof of my assets (collateral) for these obligations            .        
   The document naturally contained financial terminology and amounts            .        
 
 I understand that sending a document to myself with multiple people in CC and including external links can look like a phishing or spam attempt to an automated bot. However, I assure you this was a genuine, targeted communication to people I know personally. 
  Could you please have a human reviewer look into this false positive and restore access to my accounts (  vvsolod@gmail.com               and   slava@nansen.id              )?       
 Thank you for your time and assistance. I look forward to continuing to use signNow. 
 Best regards, 
 Vladislav (Slava) Solodkiy

tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer experienced login and integration issues with SignNow, provided detailed technical information, and received thorough troubleshooting guidance from the AI Agent. The issue self-resolved without explicit confirmation of the AI Agent's solution.
tickets.body: Hello Support Team,          I am currently unable to log in to my SignNow account and consistently receive a server error during authentication. Additionally, I am unable to send documents through the SignNow integration within HubSpot.          While attempting to troubleshoot, I captured several console errors that may help identify the issue:             A failed request to /auth/v1/sessions returning a 500 Internal Server Error with messageCode "UNAUTHORIZED"      Multiple Content Security Policy (CSP) violations related to inline scripts and styles (script-src and style-src restrictions)      Repeated "unsafe-eval" and inline execution violations      Google Analytics failing to resolve methods      Cloudflare challenge-related messages (Private Access Token + preload warnings)      A parser-blocking script (babel-polyfill) being injected via document.write      Several iframe framing violations due to CSP default-src restrictions             From my perspective, it appears there may be a backend authentication issue (based on the 500 + UNAUTHORIZED response), possibly combined with frontend script execution restrictions.          This issue is also affecting the HubSpot integration, where I cannot send documents at all.          Request ID from the error:    96db2e15c76f4358b6b0c972d6dfb7ce          Could you please investigate whether this is related to my account, API/authentication services, or a broader outage?          Let me know if you need any additional details.            Best,          Daniel Garcia-Ramirez    (503) 983-0553    Ray Reynolds Financial Services
tickets.subject: Issues Logging In & Sending Documents

tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer experienced recurring document issues via the API and received detailed but unresolved assistance from the AI Agent and teammate, ultimately opting to continue the conversation via email.
tickets.body:
Talk to human, here's transcript. Techncal error 
 Conversation with SignNow Started on May 22, 2026 at 03:02 PM Eastern Time (US & Canada) time EDT (GMT-0400) 
 --- 
 03:02 PM |  tech@tfc.com : Running into some document issues. Happened on May 14 for Malt Drive Pool Rider - Janice Li   Also happened for Malt Drive Pool Rider - Glendaliza Almonte today.   What's going on? 
 03:02 PM | Luna: Feel free to ask your question below. 
 03:02 PM | Luna: I checked our status page, and there are no active incidents right now, so this is unlikely to be a platform-wide outage.   For the “document issues” you saw on May 14 and again today, the most common cause is the PDF itself (for example: form fields, comments, layers, transparencies, or unsupported PDF types like password-protected/encrypted PDFs, PDF/A, or dynamic XFA forms). Flattening the PDF before upload (often “Print to PDF”) usually fixes shifting or rendering problems. ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates )   If you are seeing an error while opening or signing, try Incognito/Private mode and clear cache/cookies, and note your browser and version. ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13413898-best-devices-and-browsers-for-a-smooth-signnow-signing-experience )   I can help you narrow it down if you tell me what exactly happens (error message, or does the document look wrong, or can’t be opened).    Sources: Uploading Documents and Templates ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates ) Best Devices and Browsers for a Smooth SignNow Signing Experience ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13413898-best-devices-and-browsers-for-a-smooth-signnow-signing-experience ) 
 03:02 PM | Luna: Is that what you were looking for? 
 03:02 PM |  tech@tfc.com : These are the errors I got:   Exception: SignNow\Rest\EntityManager\Exception\EntityManagerException - Request failed! Reason: Client error: POST  https://api.signnow.com/document/fieldextract   [65656] Could not add fields to this document id - f5d7d4d73c23498a81515936d225558bea6d529a File: /releases/db604442-1779391722/vendor/signnow/rest-entity-manager/src/EntityManager.php, Line: 318 
 03:03 PM | Luna: That error means your API call to POST  https://api.signnow.com/document/fieldextract  failed to add fields to the document, so the document upload with field extraction did not complete successfully. ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/entities )   A few common causes to check:   - The document already has a pending invite or has been signed, which can block editing (fields can only be added to documents not sent to signers). ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields ) ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor )  - If you are uploading via Postman Web, it can fail with “Must upload one file” unless you switch to the Desktop Agent (Auto-select agent off, select Desktop Agent, install it, then retry).   If you already have the document ID and a cURL request that reproduces it, I can help you narrow down which of the above is happening.    Sources: About documents, document groups, and templates | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/entities ) About fields | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields ) About the embedded editor | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor ) 
 03:03 PM | Luna: Did that answer your question? 
 03:04 PM |  tech@tfc.com : It does it via the Signnow API or SDK or something. Look at Malt Drive Pool Rider - Glendaliza Almonte I can't even open it 
 03:04 PM | Luna: That error usually means the document is in a state where SignNow can’t add fields to it anymore, so the “field extract” upload fails. This happens when the document already has a pending invite or has been signed, because fields can only be added to documents that have not been sent to signers. ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor ) ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields )   If you can’t even open the document in the web app, it’s often a PDF compatibility issue (password-protected/encrypted, PDF/A, dynamic XFA forms, portfolios, embedded attachments, or antivirus blocking). Flattening the PDF (often “Print to PDF”) before uploading usually fixes it. ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates )   If you already have the document ID, I can help you narrow down which of the above is happening. ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/entities )    Sources: Uploading Documents and Templates ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates ) Best Devices and Browsers for a Smooth SignNow Signing Experience ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13413898-best-devices-and-browsers-for-a-smooth-signnow-signing-experience ) About the embedded editor | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor ) About fields | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields ) About documents, document groups, and templates | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/entities ) 
 03:04 PM | Luna: Was this helpful? 
 03:05 PM |  tech@tfc.com : Document ID is f5d7d4d73c23498a81515936d225558bea6d529a 
 03:05 PM | Luna: That document ID is failing because SignNow can’t add fields to it anymore, so the field-extract upload won’t complete. This usually happens when the document already has a pending invite or has been signed, since fields can only be added to documents that have not been sent to signers. ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields ) ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor ) ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13163213-editing-documents-after-signatures-are-completed )   If you can’t even open it in the web app, it can also be a PDF compatibility issue (password-protected/encrypted, PDF/A, dynamic XFA forms, portfolios, embedded attachments, or antivirus blocking). Flattening the PDF (often “Print to PDF”) before uploading usually fixes it. ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates )   I can help you narrow down which of the above is happening if you already have the steps to reproduce and the exact error you see when opening it.    Sources: Editing Documents After Signatures Are Completed ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13163213-editing-documents-after-signatures-are-completed ) About fields | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/fields ) About the embedded editor | SignNow ( https://docs.signnow.com/docs/signnow/guides-embedded-editor ) Uploading Documents and Templates ( https://helpcenter.signnow.com/en/articles/13167315-uploading-documents-and-templates ) 
 03:05 PM | Luna: Is that what you were looking for? 
 03:05 PM |  tech@tfc.com : Talk to human 
 03:05 PM | Luna: I will connect you with someone from our team right away. Meanwhile, could you please share any additional details about the issue to help them assist you more efficiently? 
 03:06 PM | Luna: Our Sales experts can provide more details on this and help you find the best solution. Would you like one to reach out to you? 
 03:07 PM |  tech@tfc.com : Yes, please 
 03:07 PM | Luna: Great! 👍  To match you with the right expert, we just need a few quick details first. 
 03:07 PM | Luna: What’s your work email? 
 03:07 PM | Luna: Roughly how many signature invites will you be sending per year? 
 --- Exported from SignNow on May 22, 2026 at 03:07 PM Eastern Time (US & Canada) time EDT (GMT-0400)
Regards   Bader Al Shehi

  12:34 PM (2 minutes ago)        
  
  
 
  
 
  
  
 
 
 
      
  to  signNow   
   
      
  
 
 
 
  
  
 
  
  
 
 
  
  
      
  
 Before I sign the new electric supply agreement with SFE Energy, I need to confirm a few things. 
 I attached my current National Grid bill. It shows that my current supplier is  Residents Energy LLC , with a supply rate of  $0.2697 per kWh . 
 I am not sure if I am still under contract with Residents Energy, or if that contract already ended and they just continued as my supplier automatically.

tickets.body: Dear SignNow Support,    I am writing to request an immediate and full refund of three unauthorized automatic renewals that were charged to my PayPal account on May 17th of 2024, 2025 and 2026, for a total amount of 288.00 USD.     PayPal Transaction details:     - 2024 (May 17, 2024): Transaction ID 1D555416D4852783Y – 96.00 USD  - 2025 (May 17, 2025): Transaction ID 637832770M3144235 – 96.00 USD  - 2026 (May 17, 2026): Transaction ID 3YY62766W3631471K – 96.00 USD     Total claimed: 288.00 USD     Important facts:     - I have never actively used your services, neither in 2024, nor in 2025, nor in 2026.  - I never received any prior renewal notification (as required by French consumer law, Article L.215-1, and the EU Directive 2011/83/EU) for any of these three annual renewals. I only received invoices after each payment.  - Therefore, none of these three automatic renewals were properly authorized. My consent was not informed and is considered invalid under European and French law.     As a result, I am entitled to a full refund of the last three renewal payments (2024, 2025 and 2026), according to the principle of répétition de l'indu (Article 1302-1 of the French Civil Code) and the 5-year statute of limitations (Article 2224).     What I need from you:     1. Full refund of 288.00 USD (96 USD × 3) to my PayPal account.  2. Immediate cancellation of any future automatic renewals associated with my email address:  moussasyllapro@gmail.com .     Please confirm the refund within 48 hours. If I do not receive a positive response within that time, I will immediately open a dispute with PayPal and file a chargeback with my bank (Caisse d'Épargne). Under EU payment regulations, my bank will refund me immediately for any unauthorized direct debit.     Thank you for your prompt handling of this matter.     Sincerely,     Moussa Sylla  PayPal account:  moussasyllapro@gmail.com   2026 transaction ID: 3YY62766W3631471K  Total amount claimed: 288.00 USD

tickets.body: Good afternoon,           My name is Chris, a member of the IT Security team here at Balfour Beatty US. We’re currently a customer of SignNow and have a bit of a weird situation that we cannot confirm. We were reached out to by a Jack Spinola ( jspinola@cahill-sf.com ) about an invitation to a workspace that was sent to one of their employees by one of our employees. They state that “ rsaravia@cahill-sf.com ” received an invite from our “ jmcadory@balfourbeattyus.com ”. Jessica, CC’d here, did not send this invite, and after reviewing her SignNow account history, we see no evidence of compromise or sending of this invite.            Attached is the original email that  rsaravia@cahill-sf.com  received, which looks like a completely legitimate SignNow invitation. We do have a workspace called “balfourbeattyus.com” as well. SPF/DMARC pass, so we know this was sent from SignNow infrastructure.           Is there any way we can receive or find any audit log information on how this invite was sent out? It was received by rsaravia on 05/06/2026 at 9:28 AM, unsure if this is CST or PST. We want to confirm if this is someone spoofing our domain using SignNow, or if the invite came from Jessica’s SignNow account and is potentially compromised?           Please let me know if you have any questions or need any more information from my end.                        Chris Sherrill         IT SECURITY ANALYST    | Balfour Beatty     C: (682) 427-9549      E:   csherrill@balfourbeattyus.com   |   www.balfourbeattyus.com                                     Confidentiality Notice: The contents of this e-mail are confidential and are intended only for the use of the recipient(s) unless otherwise indicated. tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer reported email deliverability issues with signature invites being flagged as spam or malicious and sought technical details about sender authentication and domain options. The AI Agent provided content-related advice but deferred technical questions to human support, and the customer followed up to check on request status.
tickets.body:
Hi SignNow Support,  We're a SignNow customer (Rock Rabbit) reaching out about an email deliverability issue we're seeing on outbound signature invites sent from  mail@signnow.com .  Summary Over the past week, three of our customers have independently reported that signature invite emails originating from  mail@signnow.com  are landing in their spam folders, and in at least one case the message was flagged by the recipient's email provider as "malicious." These are legitimate invites tied to documents our team sent through SignNow — not bulk or marketing email.  Because  mail@signnow.com  is a sender domain you own and control, there's nothing we can change on our DNS side (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) to address this. We're hoping you can help us understand what's going on and what options are available.  Specific questions 1. Is  mail@signnow.com  sent from a shared sending pool across SignNow customers? If so, have you seen recent reputation issues with the IPs or domain that pool uses — particularly with Microsoft / Outlook.com / Office 365, since the "marked as malicious" framing usually points to Microsoft's SmartScreen filter?  2. Do you offer a way to send signature invites from a customer-controlled subdomain (e.g.,  sign.rockrabbit.ai ) where we'd publish our own SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records and inherit our own sender reputation? Some eSignature providers offer this as "branded sending" or a "custom send-as domain."  3. If a shared pool is the only option, can you confirm the current SPF, DKIM, and DMARC posture for  mail@signnow.com , and whether there's a customer-side configuration we can use to improve deliverability (e.g., a different sender display name, reply-to setting, or branded subdomain on a higher tier)?  4. Are you currently aware of, or actively investigating, deliverability complaints from other customers in the past 1–2 weeks?  Account / impact - Account: Rock Rabbit (signing under our SignNow workspace) - Sender address recipients saw:  mail@signnow.com  - Reported by: 3 separate customers in the past week - Reported behavior: emails routed to spam; at least one inbox surfaced a "malicious" warning rather than a plain spam label - We can share message-IDs, recipient domains, and screenshots if helpful — let us know the best secure channel.  For context, I've already reviewed your KB article on preventing emails from going to spam ( https://www.signnow.com/help/prevent-emails-spam ). That guide is focused on content-side levers (subject lines, trigger words, whitelisting requests), which we'll continue to follow, but it doesn't address sender authentication or reputation — which is what we believe is actually driving these flags. 
   
 Thanks,  
 Glenn  
   
 --          
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
       
     rockrabbit.ai   
 
 
  Glenn Billman  
  Manager, Building Decarbonization & Operations  
 
 
 
   
 
  (650) 283 0124   
   glenn.billman@rockrabbit.ai
tickets.subject: Emails flagged as malicious

hi Luna  I need help with our Netsuite integration. Here is what happens (or more what not happens) 
 We send Estimate ES-38160 to the client for signature via Netsuite. The client did receive the request and signed the file. The signed PDF is available on SignNow (see screenshot: ES-38160_Status SignNow_SignNow.png) but it's not saved in Netsuite (see:ES-38160_Status SignNowNS_missing file.png).

tickets.cx_score_explanation: The customer experienced frustration with the product's billing design and company policies due to lack of clear disclosure and rigid prepayment blocks. The customer showed high effort by persistently seeking clarifications and escalation. The AI Agent provided low-quality responses, while the teammate delivered high-quality, detailed answers that ultimately resolved the billing concerns through migration to a usage-based model.
tickets.body:
Subject: Request for full plan terms disclosure — Account ID 15628117 
 Hello SignNow Support, 
 I am writing regarding my Business Annually subscription (Account ID: 15628117,  primelinea@gmail.com , invoice 0198b384-8a81-70cf-8897-99da70bb3fc0 dated 08/16/2025). 
 I have used 94 signature invites so far. My account dashboard indicates that a charge of €96 will be applied after 106 invites, which appears to be an overage / auto-renewal trigger I was not clearly informed about at the time of purchase. 
 I have three concerns: 
 1) The 100-invite-per-user limit and the €0.96 per-invite overage    rate are NOT disclosed on your public pricing page    (signnow.com/features/signnow-pricing). They are only referenced    indirectly in Terms of Service §8.2 as "units (for example    documents or transactions)", which a reasonable customer would    not understand as referring to signature invites. 
 2) The "Business Annually" plan is described in marketing as    "$8/user/month billed annually", with no mention of an invite    quota. This is a material term that affects the purchase decision    and, in my view, should be prominently disclosed. 
 3) The upcoming €96 charge will trigger before my annual renewal    date (08/16/2026), without an explicit consent step from me at    that moment. 
 I would like to request the following: 
 a) A written confirmation of the full terms of my specific plan:    - Exact invite quota per period    - Exact overage rate    - When and how auto-renewal / overage charges are triggered    - Cancellation policy and refund eligibility 
 b) Given the lack of clear upfront disclosure, I would appreciate    either (i) an increase of my invite quota for the current period    at no extra cost, or (ii) a waiver of the upcoming €96 overage    charge as a one-time goodwill gesture. 
 c) If neither (a) nor (b) is acceptable, please confirm in writing    that I may cancel auto-renewal immediately while retaining access    to the service until the paid-through date of 08/16/2026, with    no further charges. 
  ​

Please add 2 additional licenses to account, when you are able to do so.      To access your owner's portal, please visit  www.ghertner.com/owner .   For the fastest service, please submit all requests (including pool key, maintenance and architectural) and make payments through the owner portal. You may also email your requests directly to    hsr@ghertner.com   .
tickets.subject: Additional licenses required

---------- Forwarded message --------- From:  < googleplay-support@google.com >  Date: Mon, May 11, 2026, 9:40 AM Subject: Your Google Support Inquiry : [9-0924000041030] To: < gracekatie155@gmail.com > 
 
   
     Hi  Kathleen , 
 Thank you for reaching out to Google Play Support.  My name is Nova and I appreciate you for reaching out to us. I am writing to provide you with an update regarding your refund request. 
 I have carefully reviewed your request, and I sincerely wish I had better news to share. Unfortunately, the refund has been  declined  as it does not meet the criteria of Google Play’s current policies. I truly understand how disappointing this is, especially when you are looking for a swift resolution. 
  Next Steps with the Developer  To ensure you still have a path toward a resolution, I recommend contacting the app developer directly at   support@signnow.com  . As the creators of the game, they have the authority to review individual cases for their players and can often provide solutions that fall outside of our standard refund windows. 
 
 
 Response Time:  They typically respond within  3 business days . 
 
 Follow-up:  If you do not hear back from them after that time, please reach back out to us with a screenshot of your sent email, and we will be happy to look into the matter further. 
 
  Securing Your Account  To help prevent any accidental or unauthorized purchases, I highly recommend setting up  password or PIN protection  on your device. This adds a layer of security to your account and requires a password to complete any transaction. 
 Thank you for your understanding. If you have any further questions, please feel free to reply to this email. 
 Within 48 hours of our last interaction, you may receive a short survey via email. We’d love to hear your feedback about our interaction today and your overall experience with Google Support. 
 
 
   Within 48 hours of our last interaction, you may receive a short survey through email. We’d love to hear your feedback about our interaction today and your overall experience with Google support.   Sincerely!   Nova   The Google Support Team

tickets.body: Dear SignNow Billing Department,  I am writing regarding an unauthorized automatic renewal charged to my account.  Your company renewed my subscription without my consent or authorization, despite the fact that we have not used SignNow in nearly two years, as the business associated with the account is no longer operating. In addition, I had previously expressed the desire to terminate and discontinue the subscription.  At no point did I actively approve or authorize this recent renewal charge.  Given the circumstances, I am requesting the following immediately:    Cancellation of the subscription effective immediately   Confirmation that no further charges will occur   A full refund of the unauthorized renewal charge    Please treat this matter as urgent and confirm in writing once the cancellation and refund have been processed.  If this issue is not resolved promptly, I will have no choice but to escalate the matter through my financial institution as an unauthorized recurring charge as well as file a complaint with the better business bureau.  I look forward to your prompt response and your attention to this email     Sincerely,      Gianni Perrotta
